name: course-generation-method
description: How the MCAT course modules are generated from the knowledge graph
metadata:
  type: project
---

## Course Generation Method

**Source:** Knowledge graph at `wiki/.understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json` (generated by Understand-Anything from raw/ Chapter 3 & 4 materials)

**Process for each module:**
1. Read relevant nodes from knowledge graph (articles, claims, entities)
2. Map to module structure: Learning Objectives → Concept Sections → Key Equations → Worked Examples → Concept Checks → Practice Problems → Cross-Links → Summary Sheet
3. Write single markdown file to `wiki/course/module-X-<topic>.md`
4. Cross-link using 🔗 markers to other modules
5. Include Feynman technique prompts, spaced repetition schedule, progress tracking

**Module 0 created:** `wiki/course/module-0-orientation.md` (187 lines)
- Progress tracker template
- 14-day spaced repetition calendar
- Active recall/Feynman/spaced repetition learning strategies
- Note-taking template
- Self-assessment comfort rating table
- Completion checklist

**Remaining modules planned (Tasks #3-8):**
- Module 1: Thermodynamics & Free Energy
- Module 2: Kinetics & Enzymes
- Module 3: Redox & Metabolism
- Module 4: Acids, Bases & Buffers
- Module 5: Integration & MCAT Strategy
- Mastery Check & Final Assessment

**Why this works:** The knowledge graph nodes (articles, claims, entities) provide pre-chunked, verified content. Edges (builds_on, exemplifies, cites) reveal the prerequisite structure and cross-connections that become the 🔗 Cross-Links — exactly where MCAT integration questions live.
